description: When diagnosis cannot proceed, name the missing evidence, blocked reference, and next unblock action.
body: Apply when a suspected root cause depends on unavailable logs, missing credentials, external services, production-only state, absent repro steps, or erased artifacts. The action is to stop claiming certainty and record the blocked reference: which evidence is missing, why it matters, and what would unblock the diagnosis. Do not convert a blocker into a speculative fix or a broad rewrite. Verification signals include a clear artifact name, command failure, permission denial, unavailable trace ID, or explicit non-reproducible condition. Risk boundary: user-facing reports should separate confirmed facts, hypotheses, and blocked refs so follow-up work can continue safely. Use when Code Debugging work touches reproduction steps, stack traces, logs, state transitions, and controlled hypotheses. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include minimal repro, before/after trace, regression test, and command output. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves blind retries, wrapper fixes, broad rewrites, and unproven root cause, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
